description: Monitor system-wide open file descriptor count (lsof / kern.maxfiles). When approaching the limit, identify top offenders and alert the user via notification with option to kill them. Key use case: MCP server proliferation from Claude Code plugins — interkasten alone held 236K FDs across 7 zombie instances, causing system-wide 'too many open files' errors. Detection: compare lsof count vs kern.maxfiles. Threshold: warn at 80%, offer cleanup at 90%.
title: File descriptor exhaustion detection and cleanup

description: SMJobBless or XPC service for throttling root-owned processes. Covers the remaining 5% of use cases.
title: Privileged helper for root process throttling

description: Fire a notification when battery drain is abnormal. Compare current system power draw against rolling 24h baseline. Trigger at 2x-3x normal draw sustained for 2+ minutes. Include top culprit process name, CPU%, and duration. Action buttons: Throttle It, Dismiss. Only fires on battery (not AC).
title: Smart battery drain notification

description: ProcessInfo carries isThrottled and throttleTarget as mutable state that ThrottleEngine manually keeps in sync with its sessions dictionary. This is a classic redundant-state bug vector — every place that modifies sessions must remember to update the process model too. Views could derive throttle state from engine.sessions[process.pid] instead. Requires view layer refactoring since ProcessRowView and PopoverView currently read process.isThrottled directly.
title: Remove redundant ProcessInfo.isThrottled/throttleTarget state

close_reason: Research complete: hybrid model (IOReport + CPU% proxy + IOPSCopyPowerSourcesInfo). Brainstorm at docs/brainstorms/2026-03-11-battery-detective.md
description: Research the available approaches for measuring per-process and system-level energy consumption on macOS/Apple Silicon. Compare: (1) CPU% * power coefficient proxy, (2) IOReport per-cluster power telemetry (private API, used by powermetrics), (3) IOPSCopyPowerSourcesInfo for system discharge rate. Determine which approach gives the best accuracy-to-complexity tradeoff for Lowbeer. Deliverable: brainstorm doc with API availability, code samples, accuracy assessment, and recommendation.
title: Research: macOS energy measurement APIs (IOReport, IOPSCopyPowerSourcesInfo, CPU proxy)

description: Use ProcessInfo.thermalState notifications to auto-adjust throttle thresholds. More aggressive at .serious/.critical, more lenient at .nominal. Public API, no privileges needed.
title: Thermal-aware throttle aggressiveness

description: When throttling a process, also throttle its XPC services and extensions via coalition membership discovery.
title: Coalition-aware throttling
F4: Menu bar polish and system CPU display

description: Three-tier throttle: 1) E-core demotion (gentle), 2) duty-cycle SIGSTOP/SIGCONT on E-cores (moderate), 3) full SIGSTOP (aggressive). Escalate based on CPU impact.
title: Hybrid throttle strategy (E-core + duty-cycle)

close_reason: PowerSampler.swift implemented with IOReport dlsym bindings, 7 tests passing (including Apple Silicon integration), committed 866c7cb
description: Wrap Apple Silicon IOReport private API in a clean Swift interface. Subscribe to Energy Model channels, sample at poll interval, return per-cluster power (P-core watts, E-core watts, GPU watts, total system watts). Graceful fallback to CPU% proxy if IOReport unavailable (older macOS, Intel, or API breakage). Reference implementations: socpowerbud (Obj-C), NeoAsitop (Swift), macmon (Rust).
title: IOReport Swift bindings with graceful fallback

description: sampleAll() makes ~600 individual sysctl(KERN_PROC_PID) calls per poll cycle for start times and zombie filtering. A single sysctl(KERN_PROC_ALL) returns all kinfo_proc records in one syscall, giving start times, zombie status, and process names. Would eliminate ~600 syscalls per 3s cycle. Also hoist the 4KB path buffer allocation out of the loop (currently ~2.4MB transient heap allocations per poll).
title: Replace per-PID sysctl with KERN_PROC_ALL batch call in ProcessSnapshot

description: Replace the current basic popover with an integrated battery diagnostic view. Shows: (1) current system wattage from IOReport, (2) top energy offenders ranked by estimated Wh with throttle status badges, (3) daily battery savings estimate, (4) per-process one-click throttle/resume actions. Subsumes Lowbeer-dni (offender leaderboard) and Lowbeer-squ (battery savings counter).
title: Battery detective popover view

description: New ThrottleAction: demote process to background QoS (E-cores only) using taskpolicy -b -p <pid>. Gentler than SIGSTOP — process keeps running at ~1/3 power. Restore with taskpolicy -B -p <pid>. See docs/research/apple-silicon-throttling.md.
title: E-core demotion via taskpolicy -b

description: poll() runs on the main thread via Timer.scheduledTimer, blocking the main RunLoop during ~600+ syscalls and string allocations (10-50ms). Should move sampleAll() and CPU delta computation to a background queue, dispatching only the final state update to main (already done). Would eliminate micro-stutters in the menu bar UI.
title: Move ProcessMonitor.poll() to background queue

description: Use private IOReport APIs (as macmon does) for real-time per-cluster power measurement. Show actual watts saved. Risk: private API may break between macOS versions.
title: IOReport power telemetry (experimental)

description: Full integrated battery diagnostic experience: (1) Menu bar shows current estimated power draw (e.g., '4.2W' or color-coded dot), (2) Smart notification fires when battery drain is abnormal ('draining 3x faster than usual, top culprit: node'), (3) Clicking menu bar opens battery detective view showing top energy offenders ranked by estimated Wh, what Lowbeer did about each, and one-click throttle actions. Combines and replaces the separate offender-leaderboard (Lowbeer-dni) and battery-savings-counter (Lowbeer-squ) beads into one coherent user-facing answer to 'why is my battery dying?'
title: Battery detective: integrated 'why is my battery dying?' experience

description: Debounce rapid SIGSTOP/SIGCONT signals to the same PID within a short window.
title: SIGSTOP debounce for rapid PID events

description: Rewrite verifyProcess() to check (pid, startTime) via sysctl. Fail closed: deactivate if sysctl fails or start time differs. Keep name check as defense-in-depth.
title: F3: Start-time verified signal dispatch in ThrottleSession
